events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a field-service visit where the HVAC-R system was charged to "beer-can cold" instead of using proper subcooling/superheat targets from manufacturer charging charts. This is an improper charging method - "beer-can cold" is a rule-of-thumb method where someone touches the suction line and charges until it feels cold like a beer can, rather than using proper instrumentation and OEM charging charts.

This is non-compliant work. The system is in-service. The fault is improper charge - likely overcharged or undercharged, but the core issue is non-compliant charging method.

Decision: fail (non-compliant work)
Risk: medium-high (improper charge can damage compressor, reduce efficiency, cause failures)
Work stage: in-service
Component conditions: non-compliant, possibly degraded
Percent complete: 100 (it's in service)
Confidence: high

Let me write the JSON.

    "System charged by 'beer-can cold' feel method instead of OEM subcooling/superheat targets",
    "No use of manufacturer charging charts to verify proper refrigerant charge",
    "Refrigerant charge quantity unverified and likely incorrect, risking compressor damage and inefficient operation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recover and weigh-in correct charge per OEM charging charts using subcooling (TXV) or superheat (fixed orifice) method",
    "Verify metering device type and apply correct charging procedure",
    "Record actual subcooling/superheat readings and compare to OEM chart at measured indoor/outdoor conditions",
    "Check for compressor damage or oil return issues from prolonged mischarge"
  ],
  "rationale": "Charging by suction-line touch ('beer-can cold') is a non-compliant rule-of-thumb method that ignores OEM subcooling/superheat targets. With manufacturer charging charts on hand, the correct procedure is to measure pressures/temperatures and compare to chart values. An unverified charge in an operating system risks undercharge (low capacity, compressor overheating) or overcharge (floodback, high head pressure, compressor damage), so the work fails compliance and the system is at elevated risk.",
  "references": [
    "mfr charging charts",
    "OEM service information / installation manual charging procedure"
  ]
}
